heading: Boxing matches in States without boxing commissions
label: 6303 Boxing matches in States without boxing commissions
notes: Amendments2000— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a) and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 106–210
Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as a note under .Jan. 1, 1997section 23 of Pub. L. 104–272section 6301 of this title
amendments: Amendments2000— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a) and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 106–210

children.label: a
children.text: No person may arrange, promote, organize, produce, or fight in a professional boxing match held in a State that does not have a boxing commission unless the match is supervised by a boxing commission from another State and subject to the most recent version of the recommended regulatory guidelines certified and published by the Association of Boxing Commissions as well as any additional relevant professional boxing regulations and requirements of such other State.
the match may not be held unless it is supervised by an association of boxing commissions to which at least a majority of the States belong; and
any reporting or other requirement relating to a supervising commission allowed under this section shall be deemed to refer to the entity described in paragraph (1).
